purpose: Provide a validated registry of workflow step definitions, capabilities, and dependency metadata so orchestrators can discover executable steps without hardcoding them.

endpoints.description: List all registered workflow steps and their execution metadata.
Resolve one workflow step by key or capability.

dependencies.purpose: Load and validate local step manifest files backing the registry

sidecar_specification.purpose: Registry provider for discoverable workflow step definitions.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when an orchestrator or planner needs to discover workflow steps by key, capability, or manifest metadata.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a distributed scheduler, event bus, or arbitrary package repository.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Load step manifests into a local registry. 2) List or resolve steps through /workflow/steps. 3) Hand the selected step metadata to the workflow engine.

common_calls.description: Resolve a workflow step by key

failure_modes.condition: Requested step key does not exist in the loaded registry
failure_modes.recovery: Load the correct step manifest set or request an existing step key

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Load step manifest files through a registry abstraction, index them by key and capability, and expose lookup functions that return typed metadata rather than raw file contents.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Step registries drift when duplicate keys or invalid manifests are silently accepted, so validate aggressively before exposing the registry to planners.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep manifest loading and step resolution separate so the provider can be reused by both HTTP APIs and in-process planners.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not embed execution side effects or queue submission inside the registry provider.
